---
title: "BNO"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2026-03-16"
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
#setting up directory
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "/Users/kahei/Desktop/BNO_parliament_text/text")
```

```{r}
#load packages
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(knitr)
library(text2vec)
library(RcppParallel)
library(ggthemes)
library(umap)
install.packages("textstem")
library(textstem)
```

```{r}
#functions to extract metadata from each text files
extract_metadata <- function(path) {
  text <- read_file(path)
  name <- str_remove(basename(path), "\\.txt$")
  
  tibble(
    file = basename(path),
    text = text,
    title = str_replace_all(str_extract(name, "^.+(?=_\\d{4})"), "_", " "), #telling R what is the name of each doc from the doc name
    date = as.Date(str_extract(name, "\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}")), #what is the date from the doc name
    chamber = str_extract(name, "(Commons|Lords)$") #what is the chamber from the doc nmae
  )
}
```

```{r}
#loading text files into a dataframe
files <- list.files(pattern = "\\.txt$", full.names = TRUE) 

hansard <- map_df(files, extract_metadata)
hansard
```

```{r}
#Data Cleaning
#step 1: define party patterns
Party_regex <- "\\((Con|Lab|LD|SNP|DUP|PC|GP|UUP|SDLP|Alliance|Ind|TUV|CB|CrossBench|Crossbench)\\)\\s*$"

#step 2: define detection function for speakers
is_speaker_line <- function(line) { #Defines a function that decides whether a line is a speaker line
  line <- str_trim(line) #Trims leading and trailing whitespace
  if (nchar(line) == 0) return(FALSE) #Empty lines cannot be speaker lines
  if (str_detect(line, "[.,:;!?]$")) return(FALSE) #Lines ending with punctuation are not speaker names
  if (str_detect(line, "^[a-z]")) return(FALSE) #Lines starting with a lowercase letter are not speaker names
  
  # peer titles
  if (str_detect(line, "^(Lord|Baroness|Viscount|Earl|Lady)")) return(TRUE) #Recognises Lords and peer titles as valid speaker lines
  
  # Lord Bishop specifically (ends without bracket)
  if (str_detect(line, "^The Lord Bishop")) return(TRUE) #Identifies “The Lord Bishop …” as a speaker line even without a party bracket
  
  # "The X (Name) (Party)" — minister role lines, must end with )
  if (str_detect(line, "^The ") && str_detect(line, "\\)$")) return(TRUE) #Treats “The Minister … (Party)” style lines as speaker lines
  
  # ends with party bracket
  if (str_detect(line, Party_regex)) return(TRUE) #Any line ending with a recognised party code is a speaker line
  
  # personal name + procedural bracket
  name_with_bracket <- str_match(
    line,
    "^((?:Dr|Sir|Dame|Mr|Mrs|Ms|Miss)?\\s*[A-Z][a-zA-Z.'-]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z.'-]+){1,4})\\s+\\(([^)]*)\\)$"
  ) #Matches personal names followed by a bracket and treats them as speaker lines
  if (!is.na(name_with_bracket[1,1])) return(TRUE)
  
  # bare short name — ≤5 words, each title-cased, no brackets
  # e.g. "Andrew Rosindell", "Bob Seely"
  words <- str_split(line, "\\s+")[[1]]
  if (length(words) >= 2 %>% #length between 2-5
    length(words) <= 5 %>% 
      !str_detect(line, "\\(") %>% #must contain no parentheses
     !str_detect(line, "\\d") %>% #must contain no digits
      all(str_detect(words, "^[A-Z][a-zA-Z.'-]+$"))) return(TRUE) #Every word must start with a capital letter and contain only letters/punctuation
  
  FALSE
}
```

```{r}
#step 3: split each transcript into individual lines

hansard_lines <- hansard %>%
  mutate(
    lines = map(text, ~ str_split(.x, "\r?\n")[[1]] |> str_trim())
  ) %>% #Splits each document’s text into separate lines and trims whitespace from each line
  select(-text) %>% #Removes the original full text column since it’s now split into lines
  unnest(lines) %>% #Expands the list of lines so each line becomes its own row
  filter(nchar(lines) > 0) %>% #Drops any empty lines
  mutate(
    lines_clean = str_remove(lines, "^†\\s*") #Removes the attendance marker “†” from the start of a line
  ) %>%
  group_by(file, title, date, chamber) %>% #Groups lines by debate metadata 
  mutate(
    line_type = case_when(
      str_detect(lines, "^†\\s*") ~ "attendance", #Labels lines starting with “†” as attendance markers
      map_lgl(lines_clean, is_speaker_line) ~ "speaker", #Uses the custom is_speaker_line() function to identify speaker lines
      str_detect(lines_clean, "^\\d{1,2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}\\b") ~ "timestamp",
      TRUE ~ "speech" #Labels lines that begin with a timestamp (e.g., “14:32:10”) as timestamps
    ),
    turn_id = cumsum(line_type == "speaker") #Creates a running counter that increments at each speaker line, assigning each speech turn a unique ID
  ) %>%
  ungroup()


#check
hansard_lines %>%
  select(title, date, chamber, lines) %>%
  head(30)

```

```{r}
#step 4: Tag each line as speaker or speech
hansard_lines <- hansard_lines %>%
  mutate(line_type = case_when(
    map_lgl(lines, is_speaker_line) ~ "speaker",
    TRUE                            ~ "speech"
  ))

```

```{r}
#step 5: Assign turn IDs and collapse into one row per turn
hansard_turns <- hansard_lines %>%
  mutate(turn_id = cumsum(line_type == "speaker")) %>% #every time a speaker line appears, assigning each speech turn a unique turn_id
  filter(turn_id > 0) %>% #Removes any lines that occur before the first speaker line
  group_by(file, title, date, chamber, turn_id) %>% #Groups all lines belonging to the same speech turn, keeping metadata like file, title, date, and chamber
  summarise(
    speaker_raw = first(lines[line_type == "speaker"]), #Extracts the speaker line for the turn
    speech      = str_c(lines[line_type == "speech"], collapse = " "), #Concatenates all speech lines for that turn into one continuous text string
    .groups     = "drop" #Ungroups the result
  ) %>% 
  filter(nchar(speech) > 0) #Removes any turns where the speech text is empty


```

```{r}
#fix orphaned turns after manual check
idx_1050 <- which(hansard_turns$turn_id == 1050)
idx_1050  # confirm the repeated rows -> shows 1069 and 1070

hansard_turns$speech[idx_1050[1]] <- paste(
  hansard_turns$speech[idx_1050[1]],
  hansard_turns$speech[idx_1050[2]],
  sep = " "
) #appends the second speech fragment onto the first one, creating a single continuous speech
hansard_turns <- hansard_turns[-idx_1050[2], ] #deletes the now‑redundant second row so the dataset keeps only the merged speech
```

```{r}
#step 6: Cleaning the speaker column -> after second manual checking
procedural_pattern <- paste(
  c(
    "^Question\\b",
    "^Asked by\\b",
    "^Motion\\b",
    "^Moved by\\b",
    "^Statement\\b",
    "^Report\\b",
    "^Debate\\b",
    "^Amendment\\b",
    "^Clause\\b",
    "^Schedule\\b",
    "^Committee rose\\b",
    "^House resumed\\b",
    "^Commons Urgent Question\\b",
    "^Urgent Question\\b",
    "^Oral Answers to Questions\\b",
    "^Private Notice Question\\b",
    "^Bill Presented\\b",
    "^Bills Presented\\b",
    "^Commons Amendments \\b",
    "^Commons Reasons \\b",
    "^Maritime Enforcement\\b",
    "^Public Bill Committee \\b"
  ),
  collapse = "|"
)

hansard_turns_clean <- hansard_turns %>% 
  filter(!is.na(speaker_raw)) %>% #filtering NA rows in speaker_raw column after confirming that all of them contains only the topic/time (which is not necessary for the analysis)
  filter(str_count(speech, "\\S+") > 3) %>% # filters out any line with three or fewer word‑like tokens, keeping only real speech content rather than short names or procedural fragments, after carefully checking the df
  filter(speaker_raw != title) %>% #removes any rows where the speaker line is identical to the debate title, preventing titles from being misclassified as speaker names
  filter(!str_detect(speaker_raw, procedural_pattern)) %>% #remove any rows where the speaker line is identical to the procedural_pattern
  filter(!str_detect(speaker_raw, "^†")) %>% #removes any speaker lines that begin with the attendance marker “†”, ensuring these procedural entries are not treated as actual speakers
  filter(!str_detect(speaker_raw, "Committee Clerks|\\battended\\b")) #removes any lines where the speaker field contains “Committee Clerks” or the word “attended”, filtering out procedural or administrative entries that are not actual speakers
```

```{r}
#step 7: Check - randomly selects 20 rows from hansard_turns_clean and prints them
hansard_turns_clean %>%
  slice_sample(n = 20) %>%
  print(n = 20, width = Inf)
```

```{r}
#Step 8: extract party and name
#All party codes used in Hansard
Parties <- c(
  "Con", "Lab", "LD", "DUP", "SNP", "PC", "Green", "SDLP",
  "UUP", "Alliance", "SF", "UKIP", "Ind", "CB", "Lab Co-op",
  "TUV", "APNI", "Brexit"
)
# Parties is used for detecting speakers' parties, there's also established party_regex, but a new one helps me not to mix with other steps.
 
is_party <- function(x) trimws(x) %in% Parties #decide whether the last parenthesis in a speaker string is a party code or a constituency name

# is_party() is used for validating extracted party codes.

#Core Parser
parse_speaker <- function(raw) {
 
  # Strip [V] (virtual attendance marker)
  s <- str_trim(str_remove(raw, "\\s*\\[V\\]")) #trims the raw speaker string and removes any “[V]” virtual‑attendance marker
 
  # Extract all (content) groups
  m      <- str_match_all(s, "\\(([^)]+)\\)")[[1]] #finds every parenthesis group in the cleaned speaker string s and returns a matrix where each row contains one matched ( … ) segment
  parens <- if (nrow(m) > 0) m[, 2] else character(0) #extracts only the inner text of each parenthesis (the part inside the brackets) into a character vector parens, or returns an empty vector if none were found
  n      <- length(parens) #counts how many parenthesis groups were extracted, storing the number in n, for parser to decide which one is the party and which one is the name
 
  # Text before the first parenthesis
  base  <- str_trim(str_remove(s, "\\s*\\(.*")) #extracts the text before the first parenthesis in the speaker string—removing any trailing whitespace
 
  name  <- NA_character_
  party <- NA_character_ 

#Pattern 1: ministerial / procedural titles starting with "The " 
  if (str_starts(s, "The ")) {
    if (n == 0) {
      name <- s                         # e.g. "The Lord Speaker"
    } else if (n == 1) {
      if (is_party(parens[1])) {
        name  <- base                   # "Title (Party)"
        party <- parens[1]
      } else {
        name  <- parens[1]              # "Organisation (Name)"
      }
    } else {
      # 2+ parens — last is party, second-to-last is name
      if (is_party(parens[n])) {
        name  <- parens[n - 1]          # "Title (Name) (Party)"
        party <- parens[n]
      } else {
        name  <- parens[n]
      }
    }
    
#Pattern 2: in the Chair 
  } else if (str_detect(s, "\\(in the Chair\\)")) {
    name  <- str_trim(str_remove(s, "\\s*\\(in the Chair\\)")) #removes the “(in the Chair)” procedural tag from the speaker string and trims the result 
    
    party <- "in the Chair"
 
#Pattern 3: regular members (Lords, MPs, optional constituency/party) 
  } else {
    name <- base                        # everything before first "("
    if (n > 0 && is_party(parens[n])) {
      party <- parens[n]                # last parens = party
    }
    # constituency (first parens if 2+) is silently dropped
  }
 
  tibble(speaker_name = name, party = party)
}

#Apply
hansard_parsed <- hansard_turns_clean %>%
  mutate(parsed = map(speaker_raw, parse_speaker)) %>% #applying function parse_speaker to every speaker_raw row, and store the parsed name and party for each row in a new parsed column
  unnest_wider(parsed) #expanding the parsed column into regular columns, turning speaker_name and party into their own separate variables

#step 9: creating year column for later analysis
hansard_parsed <- hansard_parsed %>%
  mutate(year = lubridate::year(date))

#dividing them into different periods
hansard_parsed <- hansard_parsed %>%
  mutate(period = case_when(
    year <= 2021 ~ "early",   # pre-NSL + scheme announcement
    TRUE         ~ "late"     # post-scheme 
  )) 

#check
hansard_parsed %>% count(period)

#data table
data.frame(
  Column = c("turn_id", "date", "year", "period", "speaker_name", "party", "speech"),
  Description = c(
    "Unique identifier for each speech",
    "Date of the parliamentary debate",
    "Extracted year of the speech",
    "Early (2019–2021) vs Late (2022–2026)",
    "Name of the speaker delivering the speech (MPs, Ministers, Lords, Baronesses, and other parliamentary participants)",
    "Political party affiliation",
    "Full speech text"
  )
) %>%
  kable(caption = "Table 1. Overview of dataset variables")
```

```{r}
#Step 10: tokenise 
#clean
hansard_parsed <- hansard_parsed %>%
  mutate(
    # Remove timestamps
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "\\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}"),
    
    # Remove procedural brackets
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "\\[V\\]"),
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "\\[Interruption\\.?\\]"),
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "\\[Laughter\\.?\\]"),
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "\\[Inaudible\\.?\\]"),
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "\\[Official Report[^\\]]*\\]"),
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "\\[[^\\]]*in the Chair\\]"),
    
    # Remove anything starting with a bracket (catches (1)(a), (Dr, (when etc.)
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "\\([^\\s]*"),
    
    # Remove ellipsis
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "\\.{2,}"),
    
    # Remove currency and percentage
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "[£$%]"),
    
    # Remove punctuation and digits
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "[[:punct:]]"),
    speech = str_remove_all(speech, "[[:digit:]]"),
    
    # Final whitespace clean
    speech = str_squish(speech),
    
    # Lowercase to prevent the same words but one with lower one with upper
    speech = str_to_lower(speech))
  
#tokenise for analysis
hansard_tokens <- hansard_parsed %>%
  unnest_tokens(word, speech) %>%
  anti_join(stop_words, by = "word")

#check total word count
nrow(hansard_tokens)
```

```{r}
#analysis for Hypothesis 1
#i. Set GloVe parameters
  WINDOW_SIZE <- 6
  DIM        <- 50   #the length of the word vector, comparing semantic relationships -> stable is better with clearer results
  ITERS      <- 50  #small dataset
  COUNT_MIN  <- 5
```

```{r}
#ii. shuffle text (for early peroid)
  set.seed(42L)
  text_early <- sample(hansard_parsed$speech[hansard_parsed$period == "early"])
```

```{r}
#iii. create vocab (for early period)
  tokens_early <- space_tokenizer(text_early) #split on spaces
  it_early <- itoken(tokens_early, progressbar = FALSE) #feed the documents in order, without a progress bar
  vocab_early <- create_vocabulary(it_early) #scan through documents and build a vocabulary builds a vocabulary object — a structured list of all the unique words in your corpus, along with how often they appear
  vocab_pruned_early <- prune_vocabulary(vocab_early, term_count_min = COUNT_MIN)  # keep only words that meet count threshold #Keep only words that appear at least 10 times in the entire corpus
```

```{r}
#iv. Create TCM (for early period)
vectorizer_early <- vocab_vectorizer(vocab_pruned_early) #mapping words to ID

tcm_early <- create_tcm(
  it_early, vectorizer_early,
  skip_grams_window         = WINDOW_SIZE,
  skip_grams_window_context = "symmetric", #Use words on both sides of the target word
  weights                   = rep(1, WINDOW_SIZE)) #All context words within the window count equally
#Term Co‑occurrence Matrix (TCM): rows = target words, columns = context words, values = how often they co‑occur within the window
```

```{r}
#v. fit glove (for early period)
glove_early <- GlobalVectors$new(rank = DIM, x_max = 100, learning_rate = 0.05)
#x_max = how co‑occurrence counts are weighted: counts below 100 are weighted proportionally, counts above 100 are down‑weighted

wv_main_early <- glove_early$fit_transform(
  tcm_early, n_iter = ITERS,
  convergence_tol = 0.001, #Stop early if the improvement becomes smaller than 0.001

  n_threads = RcppParallel::defaultNumThreads()
)
```

```{r}
#vi. Get word vectors (for early period)
wv_context_early  <- glove_early$components
word_vectors_early <- wv_main_early + t(wv_context_early) # word vectors

#word_vectors_main = represent the word when it appears as a target word: capture global structure
#glove$components = represent the word when it appears as a context word: capture local co‑occurrence structure

#vii. save early embeddings
saveRDS(word_vectors_early, "glove_early.rds")
```

```{r}
#vii. shuffle text (for later period)
set.seed(42L)
text_late <- sample(hansard_parsed$speech[hansard_parsed$period == "late"])
```

```{r}
#viii. create vocab (for later period)
tokens_late       <- space_tokenizer(text_late) #split on spaces
it_late           <- itoken(tokens_late, progressbar = FALSE) #feed the documents in order, without a progress bar
vocab_late        <- create_vocabulary(it_late) #scan through documents and build a vocabulary builds a vocabulary object — a structured list of all the unique words in your corpus, along with how often they appear
vocab_pruned_late <- prune_vocabulary(vocab_late, term_count_min = COUNT_MIN) # keep only words that meet count threshold #Keep only words that appear at least 10 times in the entire corpus
```

```{r}
#ix. Create TCM (for later period)
vectorizer_late <- vocab_vectorizer(vocab_pruned_late) #word vector
tcm_late <- create_tcm(
  it_late, vectorizer_late,
  skip_grams_window         = WINDOW_SIZE,
  skip_grams_window_context = "symmetric", #Use words on both sides of the target word
  weights                   = rep(1, WINDOW_SIZE)) #All context words within the window count equally
#Term Co‑occurrence Matrix (TCM): rows = target words, columns = context words, values = how often they co‑occur within the window
```

```{r}
#x. fit glove (for later period)
glove_late <- GlobalVectors$new(rank = DIM, x_max = 100, learning_rate = 0.05)
wv_main_late <- glove_late$fit_transform(
  tcm_late, n_iter = ITERS,
  convergence_tol = 0.001,
  n_threads = RcppParallel::defaultNumThreads()
)
```

```{r}
#xi. Get word vectors (for later period)
wv_context_late   <- glove_late$components
word_vectors_late <- wv_main_late + t(wv_context_late)

saveRDS(word_vectors_late, "glove_late.rds")
```

```{r}
#normalise
nearest_words <- function(embedding, word, n = 20) {
  if (!word %in% rownames(embedding)) {
    message("'", word, "' not found in vocabulary")
    return(NULL)
  }
  word_vec <- embedding[word, , drop = FALSE]
  sim2(x = embedding, y = word_vec, method = "cosine", norm = "l2") %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rownames_to_column("word") %>%
    rename(similarity = 2) %>%
    anti_join(get_stopwords(language = "en"), by = "word") %>%
    filter(word != !!word) %>%
    arrange(-similarity) %>%
    head(n)
}
```

```{r}
#xii. result
#early period:
nearest_words(word_vectors_early, "bno") %>% head(20)
nearest_words(word_vectors_early, "bnos") %>% head(20)
nearest_words(word_vectors_early, "hongkongers") %>% head(20)

#late period:
nearest_words(word_vectors_late, "bno") %>% head(20)
nearest_words(word_vectors_late, "bnos") %>% head(20)
nearest_words(word_vectors_late, "hongkongers") %>% head(20)

#check
hansard_tokens %>%
  filter(word %in% c("hongkongers", "bno", "bnos")) %>%
  count(word, period, sort = TRUE)
```



```{r}
#Visualisation
# Combine nearest neighbours results - BNO
# ── Plot 1: BNO early vs late ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
p1 <- bind_rows(
  nearest_words(word_vectors_early, "bno") %>%
    mutate(period = "Early (2019-2021)"),
  nearest_words(word_vectors_late, "bno") %>%
    mutate(period = "Late (2022-2026)")
) %>%
  filter(!word %in% c(stop_words$word, "bno", "bnos", 
                      "hongkongers", "hong", "kong", "kongers")) %>%
  group_by(period) %>%
  top_n(10, similarity) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(word = reorder_within(word, similarity, period)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(word, similarity, fill = period)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(similarity, 2)),   # ADD: value labels
            hjust = -0.25, size = 3.8, colour = "grey30") +
  facet_wrap(~period, scales = "free_y") +        # CHANGE: free_y not free
  scale_x_reordered() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#336B87", "#2d6a4f")) +  # CHANGE: green not black
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.02)) +       # ADD: fixed axis for comparability
  labs(
    title = "Semantic neighbourhood of 'BNO' across periods",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Cosine similarity"
  ) +
  theme_tufte(base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        plot.title.position = "plot",
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines"),  # ADD: more space between panels
        strip.text    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
        axis.text           = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title          = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12))
p1
```

```{r}
p2 <- bind_rows(
  nearest_words(word_vectors_early, "hongkongers") %>%
    mutate(period = "Early (2019-2021)"),
  nearest_words(word_vectors_late, "hongkongers") %>%
    mutate(period = "Late (2022-2026)")
) %>%
  filter(!word %in% c(stop_words$word, "bno", "bnos", 
                      "hongkongers", "hong", "kong", "kongers")) %>%
  group_by(period) %>%
  top_n(10, similarity) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(word = reorder_within(word, similarity, period)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(word, similarity, fill = period)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(similarity, 3)),   # ADD: value labels
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.8, colour = "grey30") +
  facet_wrap(~period, scales = "free_y") +        # CHANGE: free_y not free
  scale_x_reordered() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#336B87", "#2d6a4f")) +  # CHANGE: green not black
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.9)) +       # ADD: fixed axis for comparability
  labs(
    title = "Semantic neighbourhood of 'Hongkongers' across periods",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Cosine similarity"
  ) +
  theme_tufte(base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        plot.title.position = "plot",
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines"),  # ADD: more space between panels
        strip.text    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
        axis.text           = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title          = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12))          

p2
```
```{r}
p3 <- bind_rows(
  nearest_words(word_vectors_early, "bnos") %>%
    mutate(period = "Early (2019-2021)"),
  nearest_words(word_vectors_late, "bnos") %>%
    mutate(period = "Late (2022-2026)")
) %>%
  filter(!word %in% c(stop_words$word, "bno", "bnos", 
                      "hongkongers", "hong", "kong", "kongers")) %>%
  group_by(period) %>%
  top_n(10, similarity) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(word = reorder_within(word, similarity, period)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(word, similarity, fill = period)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(similarity, 3)),   # ADD: value labels
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.8, colour = "grey30") +
  facet_wrap(~period, scales = "free_y") +        # CHANGE: free_y not free
  scale_x_reordered() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#336B87", "#2d6a4f")) +  # CHANGE: green not black
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.9)) +       # ADD: fixed axis for comparability
  labs(
    title = "Semantic neighbourhood of 'BNOs' across periods",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Cosine similarity"
  ) +
  theme_tufte(base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        plot.title.position = "plot",
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines"),  # ADD: more space between panels
        strip.text    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
        axis.text           = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title          = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12))  

p3
```


```{r}
# Combine Hong + Kong vectors
hong_kong_vec_early <- word_vectors_early["hong", ] + 
                       word_vectors_early["kong", ]

hong_kong_vec_late  <- word_vectors_late["hong", ] + 
                       word_vectors_late["kong", ]

# Find nearest neighbours for combined vector
# Need a slightly different approach since it's not a single word
find_nearest <- function(embedding, query_vec, n = 20) {
  cos_sim <- sim2(
    x = embedding, 
    y = matrix(query_vec, nrow = 1),
    method = "cosine", norm = "l2"
  )
  cos_sim %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rownames_to_column("word") %>%
    rename(similarity = 2) %>%
    anti_join(get_stopwords(language = "en"), by = "word") %>%
    filter(!word %in% c("hong", "kong", "kongers", "kongs", "bno", "bnos", "hongkongers")) %>%
    arrange(-similarity) %>%
    head(n)
}

# Early vs late
hongkong_early <- find_nearest(word_vectors_early, hong_kong_vec_early)
hongkong_late <- find_nearest(word_vectors_late,  hong_kong_vec_late)

hongkong_early
hongkong_late
```

```{r}
#Hongkong plot
p4 <- bind_rows(
  hongkong_early %>% mutate(period = "Early (2019-2021)"),
  hongkong_late  %>% mutate(period = "Late (2022-2026)")
) %>%
  filter(!word %in% c(stop_words$word, "bno", "bnos",
                      "hongkongers", "hong", "kong", "kongers")) %>%
  group_by(period) %>%
  top_n(10, similarity) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(word = reorder_within(word, similarity, period)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(word, similarity, fill = period)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(similarity, 3)),   # ADD: value labels
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.8, colour = "grey30") +
  facet_wrap(~period, scales = "free_y") +        # CHANGE: free_y not free
  scale_x_reordered() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#336B87", "#2d6a4f")) +  # CHANGE: green not black
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.9)) +       # ADD: fixed axis for comparability
  labs(
    title = "Semantic neighbourhood of 'Hong Kong' across periods",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Cosine similarity"
  ) +
  theme_tufte(base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        plot.title.position = "plot",
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines"),  # ADD: more space between panels
        strip.text    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
        axis.text           = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title          = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12))          

p4
```

```{r}
#UMAP for overview
#Full corpus — train on all speeches
#step 1, shuffling
set.seed(42L)
text_all <- sample(hansard_parsed$speech)

#step 2, create vocab
tokens_all       <- space_tokenizer(text_all)
it_all           <- itoken(tokens_all, progressbar = FALSE)
vocab_all        <- create_vocabulary(it_all)
vocab_pruned_all <- prune_vocabulary(vocab_all, term_count_min = COUNT_MIN)

#step 3, Create TCM
vectorizer_all <- vocab_vectorizer(vocab_pruned_all)
tcm_all <- create_tcm(
  it_all, vectorizer_all,
  skip_grams_window         = WINDOW_SIZE,
  skip_grams_window_context = "symmetric",
  weights                   = rep(1, WINDOW_SIZE)
)

#step 4, fit glove
glove_all <- GlobalVectors$new(rank = DIM, x_max = 100, learning_rate = 0.05)
wv_main_all <- glove_all$fit_transform(
  tcm_all, n_iter = ITERS,
  convergence_tol = 0.001,
  n_threads = RcppParallel::defaultNumThreads()
)

#step 5, extract vectors
wv_context_all   <- glove_all$components
word_vectors_all <- wv_main_all + t(wv_context_all)
```

```{r}
# GloVe dimension reduction
glove_umap <- umap(
  word_vectors_all,
  n_components = 2,
  metric       = "cosine",
  n_neighbors  = 15,
  min_dist     = 0.1,
  spread       = 2
)

# Put results in a dataframe for ggplot
df_glove_umap <- as.data.frame(glove_umap$layout)
df_glove_umap$word <- rownames(word_vectors_all)
colnames(df_glove_umap) <- c("UMAP1", "UMAP2", "word")

# Plot the UMAP dimensions
ggplot(df_glove_umap) +
  geom_point(aes(x = UMAP1, y = UMAP2), colour = "blue", size = 0.05) +
  labs(title = "UMAP projection of word embeddings in parliamentary discourse") +
  theme_tufte(base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

```

```{r}
# Load your saved vectors
word_vectors_early <- readRDS("glove_early.rds")
word_vectors_late  <- readRDS("glove_late.rds")

# Cosine similarity function
cosine_sim <- function(v1, v2) {
  sum(v1 * v2) / (sqrt(sum(v1^2)) * sqrt(sum(v2^2)))
}

# Extract vectors for each term
bno_early  <- word_vectors_early["bno", ]
bnos_early  <- word_vectors_early["bnos", ]
hk_early   <- word_vectors_early["hongkongers", ]

bno_late   <- word_vectors_late["bno", ]
bnos_late   <- word_vectors_late["bnos", ]
hk_late    <- word_vectors_late["hongkongers", ]

# Calculate similarity
#BNO and hongkongers
cosine_sim(bno_early, hk_early)
cosine_sim(bno_late, hk_late)

# BNOs vs Hongkongers
cosine_sim(bnos_early, hk_early)
cosine_sim(bnos_late, hk_late)

# BNOs vs BNO
cosine_sim(bnos_early, bno_early)
cosine_sim(bnos_late, bno_late)
```
```{r}
#visualise the cosine similarity
cosine_table <- data.frame(
  Comparison = c("BNO ~ Hongkongers", 
                 "BNOs ~ Hongkongers",
                 "BNOs ~ BNO"),
  Early = c(round(cosine_sim(bno_early, hk_early), 3),
            round(cosine_sim(bnos_early, hk_early), 3),
            round(cosine_sim(bnos_early, bno_early), 3)),
  Late  = c(round(cosine_sim(bno_late, hk_late), 3),
            round(cosine_sim(bnos_late, hk_late), 3),
            round(cosine_sim(bnos_late, bno_late), 3))
) %>%
  mutate(Change = round(Late - Early, 3),
         Change = ifelse(Change > 0, paste0("+", Change), as.character(Change)))

kable(cosine_table,
      col.names = c("Term Pair", "Early (2019-2021)", 
                    "Late (2022-2026)", "Change"),
      caption = "Table 3: Cross-term cosine similarity across periods",
      align = c("l", "c", "c", "c")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE,
                position = "center") %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE) %>%
  footnote(general = "Cosine similarity ranges from -1 to 1; higher values indicate greater semantic proximity.",
           general_title = "Note:")

```


```{r}
#word frequency table
# Get all four terms at once
terms <- c("bno", "bnos", "hongkongers")

vocab_pruned_early[vocab_pruned_early$term %in% terms, ]
vocab_pruned_late[vocab_pruned_late$term %in% terms, ]
```

```{r}
#visualise
library(kableExtra)

freq_table <- data.frame(
  Term = c("BNO", "BNOs", "Hongkongers"),
  Early_term = c(324, 97, 88),
  Early_doc = c(184, 67, 55),
  Late_term = c(504, 56, 404),
  Late_doc = c(228, 48, 169)
)

kable(freq_table,
      col.names = c("Term", "Term count", "Doc count", 
                    "Term count", "Doc count"),
      caption = "Table 2: Term frequencies across periods",
      align = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                full_width = FALSE,
                position = "center") %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1,
                     "Early (2019–2021)" = 2,
                     "Late (2022–2026)" = 2)) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  row_spec(2, background = "#f5f5f5") %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE) %>%
  footnote(general = "Term count = total occurrences; Doc count = number of speeches containing term.",
           general_title = "Note:")
```

```{r}
#keyword-in-context to check the meaning og watch in Hong Kong finding
library(quanteda)

# Split into periods first
hansard_late <- hansard_parsed %>% 
  filter(date >= as.Date("2022-01-01"))

# Create quanteda corpus and tokens
corpus_late_q <- corpus(hansard_late, text_field = "speech")
tokens_late_q <- tokens(corpus_late_q)

# KWIC result
kwic(tokens_late_q, pattern = "watch", window = 5)
```
